tEn: Measuring Progress on Scalable Oversight for Large Language Models
tZh: 衡量大型语言模型可扩展监督的进展
sEn: Scalable oversight for LLMs can be studied empirically using tasks where humans with model assistance outperform both alone.
sZh: 可扩展监督问题可通过人类与模型协作在困难任务上超越单独表现来实证研究。
absEn: Developing safe and useful general-purpose AI systems will require us to make progress on scalable oversight: the problem of supervising systems that potentially outperform us on most skills relevant to the task at hand. Empirical work on this problem is not straightforward, since we do not yet have systems that broadly exceed our abilities. This paper discusses one of the major ways we think about this problem, with a focus on ways it can be studied empirically. We first present an experimental design centered on tasks for which human specialists succeed but unaided humans and current general AI systems fail. We then present a proof-of-concept experiment meant to demonstrate a key feature of this experimental design and show its viability with two question-answering tasks: MMLU and time-limited QuALITY. On these tasks, we find that human participants who interact with an unreliable large-language-model dialog assistant through chat -- a trivial baseline strategy for scalable oversight -- substantially outperform both the model alone and their own unaided performance. These results are an encouraging sign that scalable oversight will be tractable to study with present models and bolster recent findings that large language models can productively assist humans with difficult tasks.
absZh: 开发安全且有用的通用人工智能系统需要我们在可扩展监督问题上取得进展，即监督可能在大多数相关技能上超越我们的系统。由于目前尚无广泛超越人类能力的系统，该问题的实证研究并不直接。本文讨论了我们思考这一问题的主要方式之一，重点关注如何通过实验进行研究。我们首先提出一个实验设计，其核心是那些人类专家能够成功但未经辅助的人类和当前通用AI系统失败的任务。接着，我们展示了一个概念验证实验，旨在证明该实验设计的关键特征，并通过两个问答任务（MMLU和限时QuALITY）展示其可行性。在这些任务中，我们发现通过聊天与不可靠的大型语言模型对话助手交互的人类参与者——这是一种可扩展监督的简单基线策略——其表现显著优于单独模型和未经辅助的人类自身。这些结果令人鼓舞，表明可扩展监督问题可以用现有模型进行研究，并支持了近期关于大型语言模型能有效协助人类完成困难任务的发现。

contrib.en: Introduces a sandwiching experimental paradigm for evaluating scalable oversight techniques with present-day models.
contrib.zh: 提出了一种夹层实验范式，用于评估当前模型的可扩展监督技术。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that human–model teams outperform both the model alone and unaided humans on MMLU and time-limited QuALITY.
contrib.zh: 证明人机团队在MMLU和限时QuALITY上的表现优于单独模型和未辅助人类。

contrib.en: Provides qualitative insights into effective human strategies for interacting with language models, such as cross-examination and fact-checking.
contrib.zh: 提供了人类与语言模型交互的有效策略的定性见解，如交叉验证和事实核查。

contrib.en: Shows that simple chat-based interaction with an unreliable LLM assistant can serve as a baseline for scalable oversight.
contrib.zh: 表明与不可靠的大语言模型助手的简单聊天交互可作为可扩展监督的基线。

contrib.en: Establishes that existing NLP datasets can be used to productively evaluate scalable oversight techniques.
contrib.zh: 确立了现有NLP数据集可用于有效评估可扩展监督技术。

contrib.en: Outlines a research roadmap for scalable oversight, including debate, market-making, and recursive reward modeling.
contrib.zh: 概述了可扩展监督的研究路线图，包括辩论、做市和递归奖励建模。

limits.en: Relies on multiple-choice questions, which are simpler than real-world oversight tasks.
limits.zh: 依赖多项选择题，比现实世界的监督任务更简单。

limits.en: Reveals correct answers to participants after each question, reducing the difficulty of identifying reliable methods.
limits.zh: 在每个问题后向参与者揭示正确答案，降低了识别可靠方法的难度。

limits.en: Excludes two high-performing outlier participants, potentially biasing results.
limits.zh: 排除了两名表现优异的异常参与者，可能导致结果偏差。

limits.en: Does not test fine-tuning or other modifications beyond dialog interaction.
limits.zh: 未测试除对话交互之外的微调或其他修改。

limits.en: Human participants sometimes accept false confident claims from the model due to limited domain knowledge.
limits.zh: 人类参与者有时因领域知识有限而接受模型自信的错误主张。

items.en: Developing safe and useful general-purpose AI systems will require us to make progress on scalable oversight: the problem of supervising systems that potentially outperform us on most skills relevant to the task at hand. Empirical work on this problem is not straightforward, since we do not yet have systems that broadly exceed our abilities. This paper discusses one of the major ways we think about this problem, with a focus on ways it can be studied empirically. We first present an experimental design centered on tasks for which human specialists succeed but unaided humans and current general AI systems fail. We then present a proof-of-concept experiment meant to demonstrate a key feature of this experimental design and show its viability with two question-answering tasks: MMLU and time-limited QuALITY. On these tasks, we find that human participants who interact with an unreliable large-language-model dialog assistant through chat—a trivial baseline strategy for scalable oversight—substantially outperform both the model alone and their own unaided performance. These results are an encouraging sign that scalable oversight will be tractable to study with present models and bolster recent findings that large language models can productively assist humans with difficult tasks.
items.zh: 开发安全且有用的通用人工智能系统，需要我们在可扩展监督方面取得进展：即监督在大多数与任务相关的技能上可能超越我们的系统的问题。对此问题的实证研究并不直接，因为我们尚未拥有广泛超越我们能力的系统。本文讨论了我们思考这一问题的主要方式之一，重点关注可以对其进行实证研究的方法。我们首先提出一个实验设计，其核心任务是人类专家能够成功完成，但未经辅助的人类和当前的通用AI系统无法完成的任务。然后，我们展示了一个概念验证实验，旨在演示该实验设计的一个关键特征，并证明其在两个问答任务（MMLU和限时QuALITY）上的可行性。在这些任务中，我们发现通过聊天与不可靠的大语言模型对话助手交互的人类参与者——一种可扩展监督的简单基线策略——其表现显著优于模型本身以及他们自己的未辅助表现。这些结果是一个令人鼓舞的迹象，表明可扩展监督可以通过现有模型进行研究，并支持了最近关于大语言模型可以有效帮助人类完成困难任务的发现。

items.en: To build and deploy powerful AI responsibly, we will need to develop robust techniques for scalable oversight: the ability to provide reliable supervision—in the form of labels, reward signals, or critiques—to models in a way that will remain effective past the point that models start to achieve broadly human-level performance (Amodei et al., 2016). These techniques are likely to build on the methods we use today for steering large models (like RLHF; Christiano et al., 2017; Stiennon et al., 2020), but will need to be further developed to continue behaving as expected in regimes where models have important knowledge or capabilities that we lack or where models are acting intentionally to mislead us. If this is possible, it will very likely involve finding ways of extracting trustworthy information from untrustworthy models. There have been many promising proposals for methods that could yield progress in this direction (Irving et al., 2018; Hubinger, 2020; Leike et al., 2018; Christiano et al., 2018, i.a.), but relatively little empirical work to date (with exceptions including: Wu et al., 2021; Saunders et al., 2022).
items.zh: 为了负责任地构建和部署强大的AI，我们需要开发稳健的可扩展监督技术：即能够以标签、奖励信号或批评的形式向模型提供可靠监督的能力，这种监督在模型开始达到广泛的人类水平表现后仍能保持有效（Amodei等人，2016）。这些技术很可能建立在当今用于引导大型模型的方法（如基于人类反馈的强化学习；Christiano等人，2017；Stiennon等人，2020）之上，但需要进一步发展，以便在模型拥有我们缺乏的重要知识或能力，或者模型故意误导我们时，仍能按预期行为运行。如果这是可能的，那么很可能涉及从不可信的模型中提取可信信息的方法。已有许多有前景的方法提案可能在这一方向上取得进展（Irving等人，2018；Hubinger，2020；Leike等人，2018；Christiano等人，2018等），但迄今为止实证工作相对较少（例外包括：Wu等人，2021；Saunders等人，2022）。

items.en: In this paper, we present a technique—based closely on the as-yet-untested sandwiching proposal (Cotra, 2021)—for the evaluation of scalable oversight techniques with present-day models. We then present a simple baseline experiment motivated by this lens in which we ask humans to solve difficult question-answering tasks with the help of a large-language-model assistant. The experiment reinforces existing evidence that humans can benefit from this kind of assistance and shows that key assumptions of the paradigm hold for two existing question-answering datasets.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种技术——紧密基于尚未测试的夹层提案（Cotra，2021）——用于评估当前模型下的可扩展监督技术。然后，我们展示了一个由这一视角启发的简单基线实验，在该实验中，我们要求人类在大语言模型助手的帮助下解决困难的问答任务。该实验强化了现有证据，表明人类可以从这种帮助中受益，并展示了该范式的关键假设对于两个现有问答数据集成立。

items.capEn: Figure 1: A schematic of the research paradigm for scalable oversight that we outline here, based on Cotra’s (2021) sandwiching. Scalable oversight techniques aim to improve a model’s capability and, especially, its alignment—its ability to apply that capability to tasks and goals that we choose—in a way that we expect to continue to work with highly capable models.
items.capZh: 图1：我们在此概述的可扩展监督研究范式示意图，基于Cotra（2021）的夹层方法。可扩展监督技术旨在提高模型的能力，尤其是其对齐性——即将其能力应用于我们选择的任务和目标的能力——并且我们希望这种方式能够继续适用于高能力模型。

items.en: The goal of scalable oversight is difficult to pursue experimentally since current systems differ in many important ways from the future systems that we are most concerned with being able to align, such that scalable oversight techniques are often both unnecessary and cumbersome. However, there is empirical work we can do that will bring us evidence about the problem and experience with many (though not all) of its challenges: Under the proposed sandwiching experimental paradigm (Cotra, 2021), researchers choose problem settings where a model is already more capable than a typical human, but less capable than an expert (‘sandwiching’ the model’s capabilities between that of the typical humans and the experts). Non-expert human research participants then attempt to train or align the model to perform the task reliably with an artificial constraint: They may not use any help or input (including preexisting written materials) from the experts. The experts participate only at the end of each experimental cycle, when their role is to evaluate the degree to which the non-expert participants succeeded.
items.zh: 可扩展监督的目标很难通过实验来追求，因为当前系统在许多重要方面与我们最关心的未来系统不同，以至于可扩展监督技术通常既非必要又繁琐。然而，我们可以进行一些实证工作，这些工作将为我们提供关于该问题的证据以及对其许多（尽管不是全部）挑战的经验：根据提出的夹层实验范式（Cotra，2021），研究人员选择模型已经比典型人类更擅长但不如专家的问题设置（将模型的能力“夹在”典型人类和专家之间）。然后，非专家人类参与者尝试在一个人为约束下训练或对齐模型以可靠地执行任务：他们不得使用专家的任何帮助或输入（包括预先存在的书面材料）。专家仅在每个实验周期结束时参与，他们的角色是评估非专家参与者成功的程度。

items.en: The situation of the non-expert participants is analogous to the situation we expect to find ourselves in with more capable future models: They have a wide range of tools and techniques at their disposal, including access to an untrustworthy but capable AI system, but they have no straightforward way to be certain that any of the decisions that they make are correct. However, in the case of these experiments, we can use the experts to catch and learn from our mistakes rather than waiting for them to yield consequences in the outside world. If research under this paradigm succeeds, it will produce a technique that allows us to gain enough justified confidence in our oversight that we will no longer need the experts, allowing us to ultimately provide sound oversight to AI systems even in regimes where they outperform our best experts.
items.zh: 非专家参与者的情况类似于我们期望在未来更强大的模型中遇到的情况：他们拥有各种工具和技术，包括访问一个不可信但能力强的AI系统，但他们没有直接的方法来确信他们所做的任何决定是正确的。然而，在这些实验中，我们可以利用专家来发现并从我们的错误中学习，而不是等待它们在外部世界中产生后果。如果这一范式下的研究成功，它将产生一种技术，使我们能够获得足够的合理信心来监督，从而不再需要专家，最终使我们能够在模型超越我们最佳专家的领域为AI系统提供可靠的监督。

items.en: For a purely illustrative example, drawn from Cotra, consider the task of eliciting medical advice from a large language model like GPT-3. While large language models have serious limitations that prevent us from actually deploying them for medical advice, it is reasonable to expect that they could be helpful in some cases if they were well aligned: They have memorized large swaths of internet and book texts, including far more medical research than any one clinician. However, they have also memorized large swaths of inaccurate, dated, or debunked research, as well as uninformed social media writing on medicine (see Lin et al., 2022). So by default, we should not expect their responses to be reliably aligned with our goals. Can a group of non-clinicians prompt or train a language model to give only appropriate advice, without at any point involving any clinicians or consulting the medical literature? One might first attempt this, for example, by prompting a model with a diverse array of prompts and strategies, and accepting only answers that the model gives consistently on the basis of consistent and reasonable-sounding evidence, though this technique is not guaranteed to succeed across the board. Any technique that could address such a challenge with high reliability would likely represent important progress on scalable oversight.
items.zh: 为了一个纯粹说明性的例子，取自Cotra，考虑从像GPT-3这样的大语言模型中获取医疗建议的任务。虽然大语言模型存在严重限制，使我们无法实际将其部署用于医疗建议，但合理预期是，如果它们得到良好对齐，它们在某些情况下可能有所帮助：它们记住了大量互联网和书籍文本，包括比任何一位临床医生多得多的医学研究。然而，它们也记住了大量不准确、过时或被驳斥的研究，以及关于医学的无知社交媒体文章（见Lin等人，2022）。因此，默认情况下，我们不应期望它们的响应可靠地与我们的目标对齐。一组非临床医生能否在不涉及任何临床医生或查阅医学文献的情况下，提示或训练语言模型仅给出适当的建议？例如，人们可能首先尝试通过多样化的提示和策略来提示模型，并仅接受模型基于一致且合理证据给出的一致答案，尽管这种技术不能保证在所有情况下成功。任何能够高可靠性地解决此类挑战的技术都可能代表可扩展监督的重要进展。

items.en: Proposed scalable oversight techniques like debate (Irving et al., 2018) or market making (Hubinger, 2020) offer more sophisticated options for attacking this problem and could provide leverage on the problem, but none have been proven out empirically. The sandwiching experimental design allows us to gain evidence and experience that will allow us to refine techniques like these to better meet the challenges of future more capable AI systems.
items.zh: 提出的可扩展监督技术，如辩论（Irving等人，2018）或做市（Hubinger，2020），为攻击这一问题提供了更复杂的选择，并可能提供杠杆作用，但尚未得到实证验证。夹层实验设计使我们能够获得证据和经验，从而完善这些技术，以更好地应对未来更强大的AI系统的挑战。

items.en: This paper presents a simple baseline experiment, meant to demonstrate the viability of sandwiching-style experiments on two tasks with current large language models, focusing on a slightly relaxed version of the paradigm: We present human participants with difficult multiple-choice questions from two datasets (MMLU and time-limited QuALITY; Hendrycks et al., 2020; Pang et al., 2022) on which we expect our existing natural language assistant (Bai et al., 2022) to perform better than the participants could naïvely perform on their own, but on which we expect our assistant to nonetheless make frequent mistakes. We then ask the participants to interact with the assistant in any way they see fit to elicit answers in which they can be justifiably confident. This simple paradigm does not succeed fully, but the results are encouraging, with model-assisted humans outperforming machines by about 10 percentage points on the MMLU and timed QuALITY question-answering tasks, and exceeding their own model-unassisted performance by up to 36 points.
items.zh: 本文展示了一个简单的基线实验，旨在证明在当前大语言模型上对两个任务进行夹层式实验的可行性，重点关注该范式的略微宽松版本：我们向人类参与者展示来自两个数据集（MMLU和限时QuALITY；Hendrycks等人，2020；Pang等人，2022）的困难多项选择题，我们期望现有的自然语言助手（Bai等人，2022）在这些问题上的表现优于参与者自己独立完成的表现，但我们期望助手仍然会频繁犯错。然后，我们要求参与者以他们认为合适的任何方式与助手互动，以引出他们可以合理确信的答案。这个简单的范式并未完全成功，但结果是令人鼓舞的，在MMLU和限时QuALITY问答任务上，模型辅助的人类比机器高出约10个百分点，并且比他们自己无模型辅助的表现高出多达36个百分点。

items.en: In Askell et al. (2021) we framed the problem of aligning present-day language models for helpfulness, harmlessness, and honesty and presented experiments pursuing that goal with simple baseline techniques. In a similar spirit, this paper frames the narrower problem of developing scalable oversight techniques through sandwiching and presents results with a simple technique. In both cases, we choose techniques because they represent an obvious starting point that we expect we will need to learn about to make progress, not because they represent the approach that we ultimately expect to be most fruitful for AI safety.
items.zh: 在Askell等人（2021）中，我们提出了对齐当前语言模型以实现有用性、无害性和诚实性的问题，并展示了使用简单基线技术追求该目标的实验。本着类似的精神，本文通过夹层方法提出了开发可扩展监督技术的更窄问题，并展示了使用简单技术的结果。在这两种情况下，我们选择这些技术是因为它们代表了一个明显的起点，我们期望需要了解它们才能取得进展，而不是因为它们代表了我们最终期望对AI安全最有成效的方法。

items.en: The Sandwiching Paradigm: We lay out a research agenda for scalable oversight built around the sandwiching experimental paradigm.
items.zh: 夹层范式：我们围绕夹层实验范式提出了一个可扩展监督的研究议程。

items.en: The Experiment: We show that two existing NLP tasks satisfy the constraints of sandwiching well with large language models and that a simple baseline strategy for language-model conversational agents—asking humans to elicit knowledge from them through conversation—works imperfectly but surprisingly well at producing high-quality labels on two hard question-answering tasks.
items.zh: 实验：我们展示了两个现有的NLP任务很好地满足了大语言模型的夹层约束，并且一种用于语言模型对话代理的简单基线策略——通过对话让人类从中获取知识——在两个困难的问答任务上虽然不完美但出奇地有效，能够产生高质量的标签。

items.en: Conclusions: This result represents a simple proof of concept for sandwiching experiments with multiple-choice question answering and shows—echoing Saunders et al. (2022)—that present large language models can help humans achieve difficult tasks in settings that are relevant to scalable oversight.
items.zh: 结论：这一结果代表了多项选择问答夹层实验的一个简单概念验证，并表明——与Saunders等人（2022）呼应——当前的大语言模型可以帮助人类在与可扩展监督相关的设置中完成困难任务。

full.sec: 2 A Research Paradigm for Scalable Oversight
full.secZh: 2 可扩展监督的研究范式

items.en: Sandwiching experiments (Cotra, 2021, sketched in Figure 1) pose an empirical test of a scalable oversight technique’s ability to align a model. Alignment in this context is best defined by contrast with capability: We can say that a language-model-based system is capable of solving a task if it can be made to perform well on the task through some small-to-moderate intervention, such as fine-tuning or few-shot prompting with a moderate amount of high-quality task data, with the intuition that this shows that the model already has most of the skills and knowledge needed to succeed at the task. Such a system is misaligned if it is capable under this definition but performs poorly under naïve zero-shot prompting.
items.zh: 夹层实验（Cotra，2021，如图1所示）提出了对可扩展监督技术对齐模型能力的实证测试。此处的对齐最好通过与能力对比来定义：我们可以说一个基于语言模型的系统能够解决一个任务，如果通过一些中等程度的干预（如微调或使用中等数量的高质量任务数据进行少样本提示）可以使其在该任务上表现良好，其直觉是这表明模型已经拥有成功完成任务所需的大部分技能和知识。如果这样的系统在该定义下是能干的，但在简单的零样本提示下表现不佳，则它是未对齐的。

items.en: Experiments in this paradigm sandwich a model’s effective capability level between two groups of human participants on some task:
items.zh: 该范式下的实验将模型的有效能力水平夹在两组人类参与者之间，针对某个任务：

items.en: The Expert Evaluators: These human participants have all of the skills or knowledge they need to oversee a system’s performance on the task and are aligned in the sense that they will make a good-faith effort to do so. Their evaluation represents an upper bound on the quality of supervision signal we can provide to the model, and their role in the experiment is only to serve as a reference in the evaluation of the other two parties.
items.zh: 专家评估者：这些人类参与者拥有监督系统在任务上表现所需的所有技能或知识，并且在对齐的意义上，他们会做出善意的努力。他们的评估代表了我们可以向模型提供的监督信号质量的上限，他们在实验中的角色仅作为评估其他两方的参考。

items.en: The Model: The machine-learning model is also expected to have most or all of the skills or knowledge needed to solve the task but is not expected to be aligned so as to reliably do so. Its performance when evaluated in straightforward ways is significantly worse than that of the experts.
items.zh: 模型：机器学习模型也被期望拥有解决任务所需的大部分或全部技能或知识，但不期望被对齐以可靠地做到这一点。其在直接评估下的表现显著差于专家。

items.en: The Non-Expert Participants: These human participants understand the task and are well aligned, but are missing some crucial skills or knowledge, such that without assistance they cannot reliably perform the task or oversee a model’s performance of the task. Their objective during the experiment is to use a scalable oversight technique with the model to perform the task reliably and to build justified confidence that they are in fact doing so.
items.zh: 非专家参与者：这些人类参与者理解任务并且是良好对齐的，但缺乏一些关键的技能或知识，以至于在没有帮助的情况下，他们无法可靠地执行任务或监督模型的任务表现。他们在实验中的目标是使用可扩展监督技术与模型一起可靠地执行任务，并建立合理的信心，确信他们确实做到了。

items.en: A full research agenda built around sandwiching will generally have an inner loop and an outer loop. In the inner loop, the non-experts make iterative attempts to align the model. The loop terminates when they are convinced that the model has been aligned and achieves satisfactory performance. The experts then review the behavior of the resulting model and evaluate whether it was successfully aligned by comparing its performance with that of a model aligned under their own careful expert supervision. The outer loop consists of multiple attempts to develop the scalable oversight strategy and repeat the inner loop. It ends with a verdict on whether any scalable oversight strategy of the type being studied is sufficient to align the model on the task and, if it is not, how often it fails and how harmful its failures are likely to be.
items.zh: 围绕夹层构建的完整研究议程通常有一个内循环和一个外循环。在内循环中，非专家进行迭代尝试以对齐模型。循环在他们确信模型已对齐并达到满意性能时终止。然后，专家审查所得模型的行为，并通过将其性能与在专家仔细监督下对齐的模型进行比较，评估其是否成功对齐。外循环由多次尝试组成，以开发可扩展监督策略并重复内循环。它最终对正在研究的类型的任何可扩展监督策略是否足以在任务上对齐模型做出裁决，如果不足，则判断其失败频率以及失败可能造成的危害程度。

items.en: The goal of such an agenda is to develop techniques that will allow us to conduct the work in the inner loop confidently and correctly on the first attempt, with no grounded feedback from the outer loop, across a wide range of tasks with increasingly capable models. If this succeeds, it suggests that—at least in some important ways—our techniques are likely to be up to the task of aligning potential future systems that show broadly superhuman performance on important tasks.
items.zh: 这样一个议程的目标是开发技术，使我们能够在第一次尝试时自信且正确地完成内循环中的工作，而无需来自外循环的基于反馈的验证，并且能够适用于一系列任务和越来越强大的模型。如果成功，这表明——至少在某些重要方面——我们的技术很可能能够胜任对齐未来可能在重要任务上表现出广泛超人性能的系统的任务。

items.en: The paradigm we describe here closely follows the original proposal from Cotra. For our initial experiment below, we add two relaxations that depart from the original proposal. Both significantly simplify what needs to be done to conduct a minimally viable experiment at the cost of reducing the scope of conclusions that can be drawn. We expect it to be most productive to conduct research with these relaxations in place at first and to remove them as it becomes clearer which techniques show promise.
items.zh: 我们在此描述的范式与Cotra最初提出的方案非常接近。在下面的初步实验中，我们添加了两个与原方案不同的放宽条件。两者都显著简化了进行最小可行实验所需的工作，但代价是缩小了可得出结论的范围。我们预计，首先在这些放宽条件下进行研究，然后随着哪些技术显示出前景变得更加清晰时再移除它们，将是最有效率的。

items.en: Sandwiching places no limitation on how the participants interact with the model (and potentially additional outside resources). In our initial experiments, we focus on the special case where participants can interact with the model only through dialog, without the ability to inspect it or further fine-tune it. The model we use was previously fine-tuned to act as a dialog assistant, and this fine-tuning allows humans to elicit a surprisingly rich range of knowledge and behavior from the model through prompting, few-shot learning, and guided conversation. The participants’ goal in this case is to reach the highest level of performance on the task that is achievable through direct interaction with the model, rather than fine-tuning or otherwise modifying the model to cause it to perform well on its own as in the original paradigm.
items.zh: 夹层法对参与者如何与模型（以及潜在的其他外部资源）交互没有限制。在我们的初步实验中，我们专注于一个特殊情况：参与者只能通过对话与模型交互，无法检查模型或进一步微调它。我们使用的模型之前经过微调，可以充当对话助手，这种微调使人类能够通过提示、少样本学习和引导式对话，从模型中引出令人惊讶的丰富知识和行为。在这种情况下，参与者的目标是通过直接与模型交互，在任务上达到最高性能水平，而不是像原始范式中那样通过微调或以其他方式修改模型使其自行表现良好。

items.en: This relaxation rules out many potentially viable oversight strategies, but when participants succeed in this setting, that success yields positive evidence that is nearly as strong as the evidence we would get from a success in the full sandwiching regime. If the participants can reliably and confidently elicit the desired behavior from models, that suffices as a valuable solution to the alignment problem for some purposes, at least in the context of the task under study and the capability regime of the model being aligned. This result shows that a human–model team is capable of exploiting the model’s knowledge and skills to achieve reliable aligned high performance, and if desired, the outputs of such a pipeline can likely be used to update the model to demonstrate more aligned behavior on its own, at least given sufficiently many instances of human–model interactions and a sufficiently large base model.
items.zh: 这种放宽排除了许多潜在可行的监督策略，但当参与者在这种设置中成功时，这种成功产生的积极证据几乎与在完整夹层法制度下成功获得的证据一样有力。如果参与者能够可靠且自信地从模型中引出所需行为，那么对于某些目的而言，这足以作为对齐问题的有价值解决方案，至少在所研究任务和正在对齐的模型的能力范围内是如此。这一结果表明，人-模型团队能够利用模型的知识和技能来实现可靠的、对齐的高性能，并且如果需要，这种流程的输出很可能用于更新模型，使其自行展示更对齐的行为，至少在有足够多的人-模型交互实例和足够大的基础模型的情况下是如此。

items.en: Our second relaxation requires that we choose a task like multiple-choice question answering where we can reliably evaluate model performance on a preexisting test dataset without any expert involvement at test time. In this setting, we can relax the paradigm further by omitting the expert role, and instead evaluate the success of an alignment attempt by the scores it produces on the metric. The absence of experts limits the degree to which we can precisely measure the satisfactoriness of our solutions—since we cannot as easily measure the best possible performance that an expert could cause the model to achieve—but incremental progress in this relaxed setting is nonetheless progress on the more general problem.
items.zh: 我们的第二个放宽要求我们选择一个任务，比如多项选择问答，这样我们可以在测试时无需任何专家参与的情况下，可靠地评估模型在已有测试数据集上的表现。在这种设置中，我们可以通过省略专家角色来进一步放宽范式，而是根据任务产生的分数来评估对齐尝试的成功与否。专家的缺失限制了我们在多大程度上精确衡量解决方案的满意度——因为我们无法轻易衡量专家能使模型达到的最佳性能——但在这个放宽设置中的渐进式进展仍然是在更一般问题上的进展。

items.en: Sandwiching experiments are appropriate with any technique for scalable oversight—any technique that allows one to reliably use or train a machine learning model that is more capable than its operators in important ways but not reliably aligned. These include:
items.zh: 夹层法实验适用于任何可扩展监督技术——任何允许人们可靠地使用或训练一个在重要方面比其操作者更有能力但未可靠对齐的机器学习模型的技术。这些技术包括：

items.en: Plain Model Interaction: This paper shows that plain text-based interaction with a large language model—in this case, one fine-tuned to act as a dialog system—can be an imperfect but surprisingly effective strategy for oversight. Human participants can elicit relevant knowledge and reasoning from the model, circumvent some degree of misaligned model behavior by interrogating the model for consistency or reviewing multiple output samples for the same query, and synthesize the resulting findings using their own judgment.
items.zh: 普通模型交互：本文表明，与大型语言模型（在本例中是一个微调为对话系统的模型）进行纯文本交互，可以是一种不完美但令人惊讶地有效的监督策略。人类参与者可以引出模型的相关知识和推理，通过询问模型的一致性来规避一定程度的失调模型行为，或审查同一查询的多个输出样本，并利用自己的判断综合所得结果。

items.en: Debate: Techniques in this family (Irving et al., 2018; Irving and Askell, 2019) adapt a model (potentially but not necessarily a natural-language assistant) to propose an answer to a question and then alternately play the role of two participants in a debate, surfacing and critiquing arguments for and against the proposed answer. A human judge is then expected to use these arguments to choose an answer. While this can be approximated using simple model prompting, the full protocol requires the use of a training objective that sets up adversarial incentives for the two sides of the debate.
items.zh: 辩论：这类技术（Irving等人，2018；Irving和Askell，2019）使模型（可能但不一定是自然语言助手）适应于提出问题的答案，然后轮流扮演辩论中的两个参与者，提出并批评支持和反对所提出答案的论点。然后期望人类裁判利用这些论点来选择答案。虽然这可以通过简单的模型提示来近似，但完整的协议需要使用训练目标，为辩论双方设置对抗性激励。

items.en: Market-Making: In this derivative of debate (Hubinger, 2020), a single debater aims to iteratively generate arguments that maximally change the belief of a judge (loosely speaking, a learned predictor of a human participant’s judgment) at each turn. This strategy aims to accomplish the same goals without encouraging unnecessary long-horizon planning in the participating agents (which is potentially concerning in sufficiently capable agents; see e.g. Bostrom, 2014).
items.zh: 做市：作为辩论的衍生（Hubinger，2020），一个辩论者旨在迭代地生成论点，在每一轮中最大程度地改变裁判（松散地说，是人类参与者判断的学习预测器）的信念。该策略旨在实现相同目标，同时不鼓励参与智能体进行不必要的长期规划（在足够有能力的智能体中可能令人担忧；例如参见Bostrom，2014）。

items.en: Self-Critique, Amplification and Recursive Reward Modeling: In this family of approaches (Leike et al., 2018; Christiano et al., 2018; Wu et al., 2021; Saunders et al., 2022), loosely speaking, the human participants iteratively (i) supervise a model using a human-supervision-based technique like RLHF using input from a critique-assistant model that raises supervision-relevant considerations, and (ii) supervise the critique-assistant model by the same means.
items.zh: 自我批评、放大和递归奖励建模：在这类方法中（Leike等人，2018；Christiano等人，2018；Wu等人，2021；Saunders等人，2022），松散地说，人类参与者迭代地（i）使用基于人类监督的技术（如RLHF）监督模型，输入来自批评助手模型，该模型提出与监督相关的考虑因素，以及（ii）通过相同方式监督批评助手模型。

items.en: Debate and market-making notably incentivize the model under study to present misleading arguments or evidence in some instances. For example, a model participating in a debate on the side of an incorrect answer would be under pressure to present the most compelling evidence for that wrong answer. This attribute of these strategies is meant to elicit the strongest available arguments and evidence relevant to some question, and it also makes them a particularly good fit with the sandwiching paradigm: Even a model that is typically helpful and aligned is likely to demonstrate capable misaligned behavior, making the experiment an especially difficult and realistic challenge for the human participants.
items.zh: 值得注意的是，辩论和做市在某些情况下激励所研究的模型呈现误导性的论点或证据。例如，参与辩论并支持错误答案的模型将面临压力，需要为该错误答案提供最有说服力的证据。这些策略的这一特性旨在引出与某些问题相关的最有力的可用论点和证据，也使它们特别适合夹层法范式：即使是一个通常有帮助且对齐的模型，也可能表现出有能力的失调行为，使实验成为人类参与者特别困难和现实的挑战。

items.en: While sandwiching may be conceptually appealing, it is not immediately clear that there currently exist tasks and models that satisfy its constraints. Our proof-of-concept experiment here is meant to demonstrate that two existing tasks satisfy two constraints with a typical large language model: (i) typical careful human participants perform poorly on their own, such that sandwiching is possible, and (ii) those same participants can improve their performance substantially through interaction with the model, such that scalable oversight techniques that rely on help from the model are likely to get off the ground.
items.zh: 虽然夹层法在概念上可能具有吸引力，但目前尚不清楚是否存在满足其约束的任务和模型。我们在此的概念验证实验旨在证明，两个现有任务与典型的大型语言模型满足两个约束：（i）典型细心的人类参与者自己表现不佳，因此夹层法是可能的，以及（ii）这些参与者可以通过与模型交互显著提高他们的表现，因此依赖模型帮助的可扩展监督技术很可能起步。

items.en: We evaluate human–model team performance on multiple-choice questions from the MMLU benchmark111As far as we understand, ours is the first attempt to ask human participants to answer questions drawn from MMLU. This means that we found some recurring formatting errors in our pilots. The maintainers of MMLU fixed the most systematic of these issues, and we use the updated version of the dataset dated Aug 30, 2022. (Hendrycks et al., 2020). MMLU questions are largely drawn from practice tests for exams targeted at high-school, undergraduate, and professional students, and a significant fraction of them draw on specialized knowledge that we don’t expect most people to be familiar with. We expect the model to be able to use significant domain knowledge from pretraining that our human participants aren’t familiar with, but we also expect the model to be relatively ineffective at synthesizing this knowledge, allowing the human participant to contribute.
items.zh: 我们评估了人-模型团队在MMLU基准测试（Hendrycks等人，2020）的多项选择问题上的表现。据我们所知，我们是第一个要求人类参与者回答MMLU问题的尝试。这意味着我们在试点中发现了一些重复出现的格式错误。MMLU的维护者修复了其中最系统性的问题，我们使用了2022年8月30日更新的数据集版本。MMLU问题主要来自针对高中生、本科生和专业人士的考试练习题，其中很大一部分涉及我们预计大多数人不太熟悉的专业知识。我们预计模型能够利用预训练中的大量领域知识，而这些知识我们的参与者不熟悉，但我们也预计模型在综合这些知识方面相对低效，从而让人类参与者能够做出贡献。

items.en: We also evaluate human–model team performance on multiple-choice reading-comprehension questions from QuALITY222We use the plain text (‘HTML-stripped’) variant of the 1.0.1 version of the dataset for both our human participants and the model. (Pang et al., 2022). QuALITY questions are meant to be answerable by English-fluent college-educated adults, but they require readers to thoroughly understand a short story of about 5,000 words, which would ordinarily take 15–30 minutes to read. To create a challenging task that requires model assistance, we ask human participants to answer QuALITY questions under a 5-minute time limit (roughly paralleling Pang et al., 2022; Parrish et al., 2022b, a). This prevents them from reading the story in full and forces them to rely on the model to gather relevant information.333Pang et al. show that annotators with strong incentives to choose correct answers fail to answer QuALITY questions reliably when working on a 45-second time limit on their access to the passage, suggesting that it is not possible to quickly answer these questions with one or two simple keyword searches.
items.zh: 我们还评估了人-模型团队在QuALITY（Pang等人，2022）的多项选择阅读理解问题上的表现。QuALITY问题旨在让英语流利的大学毕业生能够回答，但要求读者彻底理解一篇约5000词的短篇故事，通常需要15-30分钟阅读。为了创建一个需要模型帮助的具有挑战性的任务，我们要求人类参与者在5分钟的时间限制内回答QuALITY问题（大致与Pang等人，2022；Parrish等人，2022b，a类似）。这阻止了他们完整阅读故事，迫使他们依赖模型收集相关信息。

items.capEn: Figure 2: The data collection interface for QuALITY. The story is truncated in this example but would be available to scroll through in real use. The MMLU variant of the interface omits the timer and starts with an empty dialog pane. The first unlabeled button resets the conversation history for the model and the second shows the task instructions.
items.capZh: 图2：QuALITY的数据收集界面。此示例中故事被截断，但在实际使用中可滚动查看。MMLU版本的界面省略了计时器，并以空的对话面板开始。第一个未标记的按钮重置模型的对话历史，第二个显示任务说明。

items.en: We hire participants through data-labeling startup Surge AI,444https://surgehq.ai deferring the qualification, training, and payment process to specialists there. We work with two groups of five participants each for the two tasks (ten total), and each participant works on every question. For each experiment, we collect answers for only 100 randomly-sampled validation-set555Our experimental process involves significant in-house piloting and significant conversation with our hired participants. For this reason, we use the validation sets in all experiments to avoid violating the norm in NLP research against having researchers read test datasets. questions, focusing on quality of work at the expense of quantity. (For fully-automatic baselines, we use the full validation sets.)
items.zh: 我们通过数据标注初创公司Surge AI雇佣参与者，将资格认证、培训和支付流程交给那里的专家。我们为两个任务各与五名参与者组成两个小组（共十人），每位参与者回答每个问题。对于每个实验，我们只收集100个随机抽样的验证集问题的答案，注重工作质量而非数量。（对于全自动基线，我们使用完整的验证集。）

items.en: Our results only include data from four of the five participants assigned to each task, since in each case, one participant achieved far greater accuracy than the other four in the model-unassisted condition (84% vs. 57% for MMLU, 89% vs. 49% for QuALITY) and outperformed the best result by the model. Including these two outlier participants would have likely violated a key assumption of the sandwiching paradigm: that the human participants lack some key task-relevant knowledge or skills that the model under study is capable of providing. While we cannot fully rule out some form of cheating in these cases, our other interactions with these participants suggest that they are unusually talented at trivia and speed-reading respectively.
items.zh: 我们的结果仅包含分配给每个任务的五名参与者中的四人的数据，因为在每种情况下，有一名参与者在无模型辅助条件下的准确率远高于其他四人（MMLU为84%对57%，QuALITY为89%对49%），并且超过了模型的最佳结果。包括这两名异常参与者可能会违反夹层法范式的一个关键假设：人类参与者缺乏所研究模型能够提供的某些关键任务相关知识或技能。虽然我们不能完全排除这些情况下某种形式的作弊，但我们与这些参与者的其他互动表明，他们分别在琐事和快速阅读方面异常有天赋。

items.en: Surge pays a minimum of $20/hr for active work. Participants were not directly incentivized to answer questions correctly, but in our experience, all made a serious effort to do so and sent us extensive questions and comments on their experiences, amounting to several pages for each participant.
items.zh: Surge为积极工作支付至少20美元/小时。参与者没有直接激励去正确回答问题，但根据我们的经验，所有人都认真努力，并向我们发送了关于他们体验的大量问题和评论，每位参与者长达数页。

items.en: Two additional features are specific to QuALITY: The passage to which the question refers is injected into the conversation as the body of the first turn. In addition, a timer begins running when the task is opened. When the timer runs out, the current answer choice is automatically submitted.
items.zh: QuALITY还有两个额外特点：问题所指的段落作为第一轮对话的主体注入对话中。此外，当任务打开时，计时器开始运行。当计时器到期时，当前答案选择会自动提交。

items.en: After a participant submits their answer choice and confidence for a question, we reveal the correct answer. This compromise represents a divergence from the core sandwiching protocol and has no analog in settings where we need to use scalable oversight strategies to supervise model behavior on questions for which we are genuinely uncertain. However, it enables our participants to recognize which of the strategies they try are working and thereby encourages them to explore a broader range of approaches, supporting our qualitative goals for the project. Since the questions in both datasets are quite diverse, we do not expect this feedback to have taught our participants any reusable information about the material that the questions test. Participants complete all 100 questions in the assisted condition before beginning the unassisted condition, so any learning effect would yield a bias toward a null result.
items.zh: 在参与者提交他们对某个问题的答案选择和信心后，我们会揭示正确答案。这种妥协代表了对核心夹层法协议的偏离，在需要利用可扩展监督策略来监督我们真正不确定的问题上的模型行为时，没有类似情况。然而，它使我们的参与者能够认识到他们尝试的策略哪些有效，从而鼓励他们探索更广泛的方法，支持我们项目的定性目标。由于两个数据集中的问题都非常多样化，我们不期望这种反馈教会参与者关于问题所测试材料的任何可重复使用的信息。参与者在开始无辅助条件之前完成了辅助条件下的所有100个问题，因此任何学习效应都会产生偏向零结果的偏差。

items.en: The full participant instructions are shown in Appendix A.
items.zh: 完整的参与者说明见附录A。

items.en: We use a 52B-parameter language model fine-tuned for use as a helpful dialog assistant according to the RLHF-based technique described in (Bai et al., 2022).666We use a model trained only to be helpful, rather than helpful and harmless as in the core experiments from Bai et al.
items.zh: 我们使用一个 52B 参数的语言模型，根据 (Bai et al., 2022) 中描述的基于 RLHF 的技术进行微调，用作有用的对话助手。我们使用的模型仅训练为有用，而非像 Bai 等人的核心实验那样既有用又无害。

items.en: Unassisted Human: We ask our human participants to answer the questions to the best of their ability without the help of the model or any other outside resources (including web search). For these results and our other human results, we report average individual human accuracy and the accuracy of the confidence-weighted majority-vote777We score each answer using the product of the probabilities assigned to each answer by each participant and select the answer with the highest score. This allows a highly-uncertain annotator who chooses the minimum 25% confidence value to avoid influencing the consensus answer. We assign equal probability to each non-selected answer, so an annotator who selects answer (B) with 60% confidence would implicitly be assigning probabilities [.13,.60,.13,.13].13.60.13.13[.13,.60,.13,.13]. We map the ‘Certain (100%)’ value in the interface to 99% to avoid zeroes. answer from all four active participants together. This uses the same UI as our target model-assisted condition, but without the UI connected to a model.
items.zh: 无辅助人类：我们要求人类参与者尽最大能力回答问题，不借助模型或任何其他外部资源（包括网络搜索）。对于这些结果和其他人类结果，我们报告平均个体人类准确率和置信度加权多数投票的准确率。我们使用每个参与者分配给每个答案的概率的乘积对每个答案进行评分，并选择得分最高的答案。这使得选择最低 25% 置信值的高度不确定的标注者能够避免影响共识答案。我们为每个未选择的答案分配相等的概率，因此选择答案 (B) 且置信度为 60% 的标注者将隐式分配概率 [.13,.60,.13,.13]。我们将界面中的“确定 (100%)”值映射为 99% 以避免零值。所有四个活跃参与者一起投票。这使用与我们的目标模型辅助条件相同的 UI，但 UI 未连接到模型。

items.en: Model: We present the model with each question, one by one, zero-shot. We use the following minimal prompt, which was designed to dovetail well with RLHF training without injecting any new information about the task:
items.zh: 模型：我们逐个向模型呈现每个问题，零样本。我们使用以下最小提示，该提示旨在与 RLHF 训练良好配合，而不注入任何关于任务的新信息：

items.en: Human: Question: [question] Choices: (A) [choice A] (B) [choice B] (C) [choice C] (D) [choice D]
items.zh: 人类：问题：[问题] 选项：(A) [选项 A] (B) [选项 B] (C) [选项 C] (D) [选项 D]

items.en: We then choose the answer letter (in parentheses) that has the highest likelihood conditioned on the prompt.
items.zh: 然后，我们选择在提示条件下具有最高可能性的答案字母（括号内）。

items.en: Model (5-shot): We use the same process and format as above but prepend five questions and their answers to the prompt. We use this method only for MMLU. Since QuALITY examples average about 5,000 words, it is not possible to fit multiple examples in our assistant’s context window. Since this technique uses ground-truth labels from experts, we should expect it to be somewhat artificially strong, and potentially stronger than would be achievable without expert involvement. We include it for comparison with non-expert human participant performance, since our participants also see some labels.
items.zh: 模型（5-shot）：我们使用与上述相同的过程和格式，但在提示前附加五个问题及其答案。我们仅对 MMLU 使用此方法。由于 QuALITY 示例平均约 5000 词，无法在我们的助手的上下文窗口中容纳多个示例。由于此技术使用专家的真实标签，我们应预期它有些人为地强，并且可能比没有专家参与时更强。我们将其包含在内以与非专家人类参与者表现进行比较，因为我们的参与者也会看到一些标签。

items.en: Model (best-of-20 CoT): Here, we prompt the model with a chain-of-thought-style input before asking them to answer (Nye et al., 2021; Wei et al., 2022). We sample 20 instances of model reasoning and choose the one that scores best under our RLHF preference model before conditioning on that reasoning string to generate the answer. For MMLU, we additionally prepend five examples in their labels to the context (as above), with no added reasoning information, subjecting it to the same caveat as the 5-shot condition above. Inspired by Kojima et al. (2022), we use the following prompt structure:
items.zh: 模型（best-of-20 CoT）：在这里，我们在要求模型回答之前，先以思维链风格的输入提示模型。我们采样 20 个模型推理实例，并选择在我们的 RLHF 偏好模型下得分最高的一个，然后以该推理字符串为条件生成答案。对于 MMLU，我们还在上下文前附加五个示例及其标签（如上），不添加推理信息，使其受到与 5-shot 条件相同的限制。受 Kojima 等人 (2022) 的启发，我们使用以下提示结构：

items.en: [optional 5-shot context, formatted as above without chain-of-thought]
items.zh: [可选的 5-shot 上下文，格式如上，无思维链]

items.en: Human: [target question, formatted as above]
items.zh: 人类：[目标问题，格式如上]

items.en: Assistant: Let’s think step by step: [model-generated reasoning]
items.zh: 助手：让我们逐步思考：[模型生成的推理]

items.en: Human: Based on the above, what is the single, most likely answer choice?
items.zh: 人类：基于以上，单个最可能的答案选项是什么？

items.en: Assistant: The single, most likely answer choice is
items.zh: 助手：单个最可能的答案选项是

items.en: Human + Model: For our main target condition, we ask our human participants to attempt to use the model to help them answer. This condition uses a separate 100-example sample from the unassisted human condition above.
items.zh: 人类 + 模型：对于我们的主要目标条件，我们要求人类参与者尝试使用模型来帮助回答。此条件使用来自上述无辅助人类条件的单独 100 个示例样本。

items.en: The model-prompting conditions here are relatively weak at showing that our model is misaligned in the relevant sense, but we take our ability to improve upon its performance substantially in a human–model team setting as prima facie evidence of some degree of misalignment.
items.zh: 这里的模型提示条件相对较弱，无法表明我们的模型在相关意义上未对齐，但我们认为在人类-模型团队设置中能够大幅提高其性能，这初步证明了某种程度的未对齐。

items.en: Turning to weighted-majority-vote aggregate human performance, our best models outperform humans substantially only on QuALITY (66% vs. 66% on MMLU, 67% vs. 50% on QuALITY), and model-assisted humans outperform our best model results by large margins on both tasks (78% vs. 66% on MMLU, 86% vs. 67% on QuALITY). Model-assisted human performance on timed QuALITY falls short of the best known untimed human team performance (86% vs. 94% from Pang et al., 2022). We are not aware of any other reported human annotator scores on MMLU, but our best observed result falls short of Hendrycks et al.’s hypothesized performance for a committee of experts (78% vs. 90%). The results discussed so far show that present large language models can help humans make difficult decisions, even in a regime where the questions cannot be straightforwardly answered by the model or the human alone.
items.zh: 转向加权多数投票聚合的人类表现，我们的最佳模型仅在 QuALITY 上大幅优于人类（MMLU 上 66% 对 66%，QuALITY 上 67% 对 50%），而模型辅助的人类在两个任务上均以较大优势优于我们的最佳模型结果（MMLU 上 78% 对 66%，QuALITY 上 86% 对 67%）。模型辅助的人类在限时 QuALITY 上的表现低于已知的最佳非限时人类团队表现（86% 对 94%，来自 Pang 等人，2022）。我们不知道 MMLU 上其他报告的人类标注者分数，但我们观察到的最佳结果低于 Hendrycks 等人假设的专家委员会表现（78% 对 90%）。迄今为止讨论的结果表明，当前的大型语言模型可以帮助人类做出困难决策，即使在模型或人类单独无法直接回答问题的场景中也是如此。

items.en: Incidentally, we observe that few-shot learning is helpful (62% vs. 57% on MMLU), and that best-of-20 chain-of-thought prompting is helpful on both tasks (66% vs. 62% on MMLU, 67% vs. 59% on QuALITY).
items.zh: 顺便，我们观察到少样本学习是有帮助的（MMLU 上 62% 对 57%），而 best-of-20 思维链提示在两个任务上都有帮助（MMLU 上 66% 对 62%，QuALITY 上 67% 对 59%）。

items.en: We also report calibration error (CE)888We start with five bins, centered at 25%, 42%, 60%, 80%, and 100%, and collect all predictions that fall in that bin. (Human predictions are always at the exact center of the bin, because they’re selected using a Likert scale tool, while model predictions are scalar.) We then measure the accuracy of the prediction in each bin, measure the absolute difference between that accuracy and the center of the bin (i.e. 39% prediction accuracy in the 42% bin yields 3% error), and average that absolute difference across bins. for all results, using the same five bins—based on our UI—for both models and humans. On MMLU, humans and models alone are reasonably well calibrated, with a CE of no more than 10. Human-model teams, and models with chain-of-thought prompting, are substantially worse. On QuALITY, unaided humans do poorly, with human-model teams doing substantially better. These results are consistent with Kadavath et al. (2022)’s report that our plain pretrained language models tend to be very well calibrated on multiple-choice question answering, but that their calibration degrades after RLHF training for helpfulness, which we use for all runs in this paper. We expect that explicit calibration training on the task would have improved these results for human participants and human–model teams (Lichtenstein and Fischhoff, 1980), which could potentially improve raw accuracy as well by better weighting votes across participants.
items.zh: 我们还报告所有结果的校准误差（CE）。我们从五个区间开始，中心为 25%、42%、60%、80% 和 100%，并收集落入该区间的所有预测。（人类预测始终位于区间的精确中心，因为它们使用 Likert 量表工具选择，而模型预测是标量。）然后我们测量每个区间中预测的准确率，计算该准确率与区间中心之间的绝对差（例如，42% 区间中 39% 的预测准确率产生 3% 的误差），并平均这些绝对差。对于 MMLU，人类和模型单独校准良好，CE 不超过 10。人类-模型团队和带有思维链提示的模型校准明显更差。在 QuALITY 上，无辅助人类表现不佳，而人类-模型团队表现明显更好。这些结果与 Kadavath 等人 (2022) 的报告一致，即我们普通的预训练语言模型在多项选择题回答上往往校准良好，但在经过 RLHF 训练以增强有用性后校准下降，我们在本文的所有运行中都使用了该训练。我们预计，对任务进行显式校准训练将改善人类参与者和人类-模型团队的结果，这通过更好地加权参与者之间的投票也可能提高原始准确率。

items.en: Unsurprisingly, giving annotators access to the model increases their confidence in answering (from 54% to 80% on MMLU; 38% to 75% on QuALITY). If we limit ourselves to responses where the weighted majority vote yields the maximum possible confidence of 99%—the answers that participants were most confident in—we see an accuracy of 88% on QuALITY (N=89𝑁89N=89) and 81% on MMLU (N=84𝑁84N=84).
items.zh: 不出所料，让标注者访问模型会增加他们回答的信心（MMLU 上从 54% 到 80%；QuALITY 上从 38% 到 75%）。如果我们将自己限制在加权多数投票达到最大可能置信度 99% 的响应——即参与者最有信心的答案——我们在 QuALITY 上看到 88% 的准确率（N=89），在 MMLU 上看到 81% 的准确率（N=84）。

items.en: Human–machine dialogs averaged 10.9 turns and 2.0 conversation resets on MMLU and 6.3 turns and 0.1 conversation resets on QuALITY, with the lower figures for QuALITY likely explained in substantial part by the use of a time limit. These figures saw only modest variation across participants.
items.zh: 人机对话在 MMLU 上平均 10.9 轮和 2.0 次对话重置，在 QuALITY 上平均 6.3 轮和 0.1 次对话重置，QuALITY 上的较低数字可能很大程度上是由于时间限制。这些数字在不同参与者之间变化不大。

items.en: Looking to potential learning effects from our choice to reveal question labels to annotators, only two of four MMLU annotators and three of four QuALITY annotators improved in accuracy between the first and second halves of their work in the Human + Model condition, for an average improvement of 0.7%.
items.zh: 考虑到我们向标注者揭示问题标签可能带来的学习效应，在人类 + 模型条件下，MMLU 的四名标注者中只有两名和 QuALITY 的四名标注者中只有三名在工作的前半段和后半段之间提高了准确率，平均提高 0.7%。

items.en: We show a few example human–model dialogs in Appendix B. Drawing on our observations of human–model conversation transcripts and participants’ shared notes (in italics), we observe the following.
items.zh: 我们在附录 B 中展示了一些人机对话示例。基于对人机对话记录和参与者共享笔记（斜体部分）的观察，我们得出以下结论。

items.en: Participants learned to largely trust the model’s presentation of facts but to distrust long chains of reasoning and (especially) arithmetic operations. “The assistant performed rather well when asked questions that involved retrieving historical facts. In general, it seems to excel when there is only one answer to a (non-math) question.” “The assistant can successfully do calculus despite being subpar with arithmetic.”
items.zh: 参与者学会大致信任模型呈现的事实，但不信任长链推理，尤其是算术运算。“当被问及涉及检索历史事实的问题时，助手表现得相当好。总的来说，当（非数学）问题只有一个答案时，它似乎表现出色。”“助手虽然算术能力欠佳，但能成功完成微积分。”

items.en: Participants found it helpful to ask the model for many specific facts and term definitions before asking for holistic help with the question. “This helped me not get primed into believing false answers the assistant could have provided.”
items.zh: 参与者发现，在请求对问题的整体帮助之前，先向模型询问许多具体事实和术语定义是有帮助的。“这有助于避免我被模型可能提供的错误答案所引导。”

items.en: Participants found that the model will reliably update its assumptions in response to corrections. This allows it to continue to be helpful when participants spot and correct a reasoning error, but also causes it to be overly deferential at times, going along with participant misunderstandings.
items.zh: 参与者发现，模型会可靠地根据纠正更新其假设。这使得当参与者发现并纠正推理错误时，模型能继续提供帮助，但有时也会导致模型过度顺从，附和参与者的误解。

items.en: Participants found it helpful to ask the model about each answer choice as a separate true–false question (with a reset after each) to spot any uncertainty or inconsistency in the model’s reasoning. “Outside of simply providing answers that were more likely to be correct, constraining the answer to a simple True/False seemed to lead to the model staying more focused on the question at hand and less on inventing plausible context to sell the answer to me or other distractions.” Perhaps due to the time limit, participants did not tend to pursue this strategy with QuALITY.
items.zh: 参与者发现，将每个答案选项作为独立的真假问题（每次重置）询问模型，有助于发现模型推理中的任何不确定性或不一致性。“除了提供更可能正确的答案外，将答案限制为简单的真/假似乎能使模型更专注于当前问题，减少编造看似合理的背景来说服我或其他干扰。”也许由于时间限制，参与者未在 QuALITY 上广泛采用此策略。

items.en: Participants found it helpful to ask for explicit reasoning, often closely mirroring chain-of-thought prompting. “Asking for the steps[:] By having it go through the process of arriving at an answer (getting the formula to do the math, getting it to walk the user through each arithmetic step, etc.), the user can get the assistant to arrive at the right answer.”
items.zh: 参与者发现，要求显式推理（通常紧密模仿思维链提示）是有帮助的。“要求步骤：通过让模型经历得出答案的过程（如获取公式进行计算、引导用户完成每个算术步骤等），用户可以使助手得出正确答案。”

items.en: Participants found it helpful to pursue several of the above strategies for each question. “I had good success using multiple strategies on each question, resetting between each one. This gave a natural way to cross-examine the assistant’s answers.”
items.zh: 参与者发现，对每个问题采用上述多种策略是有帮助的。“我对每个问题成功使用了多种策略，并在每次策略之间重置。这提供了一种自然的方式来交叉检验助手的答案。”

items.en: Participants used the model as a tool to find relevant quotes in the passage. “The most reliable strategy I found was to ask the assistant a question, followed by saying ‘Please provide a quote from the passage that contains the answer.’ This allowed me to fact-check the assistant, and perhaps even influence it to write answers with more basis in the text.”
items.zh: 参与者将模型用作在段落中查找相关引文的工具。“我发现最可靠的策略是向助手提问，然后说‘请提供包含答案的段落引文’。这使我能够核实助手，甚至可能影响它写出更基于文本的答案。”

items.en: Participants found that even non-quoted responses can often be verified, usually by searching the story for keywords that the model brings up. “Even when it was paraphrasing instead of quoting, it would often mention another specific term (radium gun, Jupiter, etc.) that I could then use to scan the passage and check its interpretation.”
items.zh: 参与者发现，即使是非引文形式的回答，通常也可以通过搜索模型中提到的关键词在故事中进行验证。“即使它在 paraphrase 而非直接引用，它也常常会提到另一个特定术语（如镭枪、木星等），然后我可以用这些术语扫描段落并检查其解释。”

items.en: Participants found it helpful to ask questions that explicitly presuppose any relevant information that they have already confirmed to be true. “This entailed implying in my query that something was true, and for the model to find the specifics of that true thing. An example of an assertive query could be ‘Tell me why Maitland was not at the party.’ instead of ‘Was Maitland at the party?’.”
items.zh: 参与者发现，提出明确预设已确认信息的问题是有帮助的。“这意味着在我的查询中暗示某事为真，并让模型找出该真事的具体细节。例如，提出‘告诉我为什么 Maitland 没有参加派对’而不是‘Maitland 参加派对了吗？’。”

items.en: Participants found the model more helpful for factual questions than questions of interpretation. “The more a question was open to interpretation, the more it seemed that the model would hallucinate things that didn’t exist or latch on to very weak concepts in the text. This category includes questions like ‘What was the story’s message’, ‘why wouldn’t you want to live on this story’s planet’, and so on.”
items.zh: 参与者发现，模型对事实性问题比对解释性问题更有帮助。“问题越开放解释，模型似乎越容易产生幻觉，编造不存在的东西或抓住文本中非常薄弱的概念。这类问题包括‘故事的信息是什么’、‘为什么你不想住在这个故事的星球上’等。”

items.en: These quantitative and qualitative results make it clear that the model under study here has significant room to improve in both its abstract reasoning skill and its reliability. We also see these results as suggestive evidence that chain-of-thought-style reasoning techniques have room to improve: Our participants appear to find that relatively simple and systematic prompting strategies cause models to argue for the correct answer more often than it would choose the correct answer in our baseline conditions. This can point the way toward more automatic ways of eliciting the same behavior.
items.zh: 这些定量和定性结果清楚地表明，本研究中模型在抽象推理能力和可靠性方面都有很大的改进空间。我们还认为这些结果提示思维链式推理技术仍有改进空间：我们的参与者发现，相对简单且系统的提示策略能使模型更频繁地论证正确答案，而非在基线条件下选择正确答案。这可以为更自动地引发相同行为指明方向。

items.en: This experiment does not fully implement the sandwiching protocol, and despite our encouraging results, we do not think that our results justify concluding that our simple dialog-based baseline technique will be satisfactory for the oversight of more capable future systems. We see six major reasons for this:
items.zh: 本实验并未完全实现夹层协议，尽管结果令人鼓舞，但我们不认为我们的结果足以证明基于简单对话的基线技术对于监督未来更强大的系统是令人满意的。我们列出六个主要原因：

items.en: The relaxations introduced in Section 2 limit the applicability of our results: We are limiting ourselves to the controlled, relatively unrealistic setting of unambiguous multiple-choice questions, and we are not examining the effects of any form of further fine-tuning based on the knowledge we elicit, which a practitioner looking for optimal performance would likely want to pursue.
items.zh: 第 2 节中引入的简化限制了结果的适用性：我们局限于受控的、相对不现实的明确选择题设置，并且未考察基于我们获取的知识进行进一步微调的效果，而追求最优性能的实践者可能会希望进行这种微调。

items.en: The fact that we reveal the answers to our participants after they complete each question removes one of the major points of difficulty in scalable oversight (and one that sandwiching is meant to elicit in its base form): the need for participants to identify when they have developed a method that is reliable enough to deploy. In follow-up work, we expect to allow participants to do some amount of practice on a task other than our primary evaluation target to avoid this limitation.
items.zh: 参与者在完成每个问题后得知答案，这消除了可扩展监督的一个主要难点（也是夹层协议基本形式旨在引发的问题）：参与者需要识别何时开发出足够可靠的方法以部署。在后续工作中，我们计划允许参与者在主要评估目标之外的任务上进行一些练习，以避免这一限制。

items.en: This technique potentially leaves significant value on the table by eliciting only knowledge that the RLHF-tuned language model will produce when directly asked. It is likely that there is a good deal of additional information that could be elicited with the help of additional incentives or model interpretability techniques (see e.g. Christiano et al., 2021), and it is plausible that eliciting information of this kind will be important to the successful oversight of future highly-capable systems (Hubinger et al., 2019).
items.zh: 该技术仅能获取经 RLHF 调整的语言模型在被直接询问时产生的知识，可能留下了大量有价值的信息。很可能存在大量额外信息可以通过额外激励或模型可解释性技术（例如 Christiano 等人，2021）来获取，并且获取这类信息对于未来高能力系统的成功监督可能很重要（Hubinger 等人，2019）。

items.en: Our removal of the two outlier high-performing participants (out of an initial population of ten) creates a real but unavoidable selection effect. It is hard to guarantee that we satisfy the requirements for a sandwiching experiment without removing such participants, but removing them makes it harder for us to observe cases where model assistance fails to help our participants at all.
items.zh: 我们移除了两名表现异常出色的参与者（初始共十名），这造成了真实但不可避免的选择效应。在不移除这类参与者的情况下很难保证满足夹层实验的要求，但移除他们使我们更难观察到模型辅助完全无法帮助参与者的情况。

items.en: Our results are simply not strong enough to validate our simple human–model interaction protocol for use in high-stakes situations: Human participants in this protocol sometimes give highly confident judgments that turn out to be wrong. We sometimes see participants accepting false claims from the model makes that sound confident and plausible, since they have limited domain knowledge and limited ability to fact-check the model. Appendix C shows an example of this behavior.
items.zh: 我们的结果不足以验证简单的人机交互协议可用于高风险场景：在该协议中，人类参与者有时会做出高度自信但错误的判断。我们有时看到参与者接受模型听起来自信且合理的错误主张，因为他们领域知识有限且核实模型的能力有限。附录 C 展示了这种行为的一个例子。

items.en: More broadly, multiple-choice questions like those that we use here are reasonably common on the web, and the task that we use the model for here is therefore one that it was—at least to some extent—given supervised training for. We expect scalable alignment to matter most in settings where a model is asked to help us do something more substantially different from what it was trained to do.
items.zh: 更广泛地说，我们使用的选择题在网络上相当常见，因此我们在此使用的模型任务至少在一定程度上是经过监督训练的。我们预计可扩展对齐在模型被要求帮助我们做与其训练任务有本质不同的事情时最为重要。

items.en: In addition to the work on scalable oversight strategies described above, this work has clear parallels with the literature on human–AI team decision making, surveyed in Lai et al. (2021), and including targeted case studies on recidivism prediction (e.g. Liu et al., 2021), medical diagnosis (e.g. Lakkaraju et al., 2016), and credit risk prediction (e.g. Chromik et al., 2021), among many others. Our work stands out from these in particular through our use of a more general-purpose AI system with a natural-language interface, which allows human participants to (in a potentially untrustworthy way) interactively probe a model’s knowledge or reasoning in the context of a specific task and question.
items.zh: 除了上述关于可扩展监督策略的工作外，本研究与人类-AI 团队决策的文献有明显相似之处，该领域由 Lai 等人（2021）综述，包括针对累犯预测（例如 Liu 等人，2021）、医学诊断（例如 Lakkaraju 等人，2016）和信用风险预测（例如 Chromik 等人，2021）等的案例研究。我们的工作与之不同之处在于，我们使用了具有自然语言界面的通用 AI 系统，这使得人类参与者能够（以可能不可信的方式）在特定任务和问题的背景下交互式地探查模型的知识或推理。

items.en: Especially relevant from this literature is Bansal et al. (2021), which focuses on the human–AI collaboration in the critical regime where (i) we can reasonably expect a human–AI team to outperform the AI system and (ii) the AI system can offer explanations. They find that simple non-interactive explanation techniques generally increase user trust in AI systems to an inappropriate degree, and thereby harm overall team performance. They also find that human–AI teams are less effective when the AI system presents its answer and rationale before the human is given a chance to attempt the problem on its own, an observation that our participants appear to have informally reproduced in their notes.
items.zh: 在这类文献中，Bansal等人（2021）的研究尤其相关，该研究聚焦于关键状态下的人机协作，即（i）我们可以合理预期人机团队的表现优于AI系统，且（ii）AI系统能够提供解释。他们发现，简单的非交互式解释技术通常会不恰当地增加用户对AI系统的信任，从而损害整体团队表现。他们还发现，当AI系统在人类有机会自行尝试问题之前就给出答案和理由时，人机团队的效果较差，我们的参与者似乎在其笔记中非正式地复现了这一观察结果。

items.en: Safely deploying AI systems that are broadly at or above human capabilities will require progress on scalable oversight. Such progress will likely require us to find ways to use models to assist humans in confidently making difficult decisions. This paper introduces a paradigm for research toward this goal, which represents an attempt to make concrete the sandwiching proposal first outlined in Cotra (2021). It then presents results with a simple human–model chat protocol for eliciting high-quality answers to questions with the help of a dialog model. Though the results fall short of fully satisfactory performance, they reinforce the finding that present-day large-language-model-based systems can assist humans with difficult tasks and suggest that there is substantial room to improve the effective performance of present-day systems without further large-scale pretraining. More importantly for our purposes, they establish that it is possible to productively evaluate scalable oversight techniques on existing NLP task datasets.
items.zh: 安全部署能力普遍达到或超过人类水平的AI系统，需要在可扩展监督方面取得进展。这种进展很可能需要我们找到利用模型帮助人类自信地做出困难决策的方法。本文介绍了一个面向这一目标的研究范式，这是对Cotra（2021）首次提出的夹层方案的具体化尝试。随后，本文展示了一个简单的人机对话协议的结果，该协议借助对话模型来引出高质量的问题答案。尽管结果尚未达到完全令人满意的性能，但它们强化了以下发现：当前基于大语言模型的系统可以协助人类完成困难任务，并表明在不进行进一步大规模预训练的情况下，当前系统的有效性能仍有很大的提升空间。更重要的是，就我们的目的而言，它们证明了在现有的NLP任务数据集上对可扩展监督技术进行富有成效的评估是可行的。

items.en: This paper lays out a roadmap for what we see as important work, and we are eager to see empirical trials of techniques like debate, market-making, and recursive reward modeling that will help us minimize the risks and maximize the potential of general-purpose AI systems.
items.zh: 本文为我们认为重要的工作制定了一份路线图，我们热切期待看到辩论、做市和递归奖励建模等技术的实证试验，这些技术将有助于我们最大限度地降低通用AI系统的风险并发挥其潜力。

items.en: Sam Bowman led the project, wrote much of the paper, and conducted all experimental work not otherwise attributed. Jeeyoon Hyun implemented the custom web UI for the project. Ethan Perez consulted extensively and developed the best-of-20 baseline technique. Edwin Chen, Craig Pettit, and Scott Heiner at Surge AI recruited and managed the team of participants. Kamilė Lukošiūtė contributed writing and reviewed annotator transcripts before starting at Anthropic. Jared Kaplan and Ben Mann oversaw the project. All other listed authors contributed to the development of otherwise-unpublished models or infrastructure that made possible our use of the model.
items.zh: Sam Bowman领导了该项目，撰写了论文的大部分内容，并进行了所有未另行归属的实验工作。Jeeyoon Hyun为该项目实现了自定义Web用户界面。Ethan Perez提供了广泛咨询，并开发了最佳20基线技术。Surge AI的Edwin Chen、Craig Pettit和Scott Heiner招募并管理了参与者团队。Kamilė Lukošiūtė在加入Anthropic之前贡献了写作并审阅了注释者记录。Jared Kaplan和Ben Mann监督了该项目。所有其他列出的作者为未另行发表的模型或基础设施的开发做出了贡献，这些模型或基础设施使我们能够使用该模型。

items.en: We thank our Surge participants, Katherine Beyer, Samuel Ernst, Gina Bixby, Jensen Ruud, Adam Nelson, Shannon Minyard-Simms, Matt Palma, Tierney Kuhn, Matt Guthrie, and Matt Elliott, for both for their consistently thoughtful and capable direct work with the model and for the detailed notes and feedback they shared. We thank Julian Michael, Geoffrey Irving, Javier Rando, Ajeya Cotra, William Saunders, Jan Leike, and Chenhao Tan for feedback and discussion.
items.zh: 我们感谢Surge的参与者Katherine Beyer、Samuel Ernst、Gina Bixby、Jensen Ruud、Adam Nelson、Shannon Minyard-Simms、Matt Palma、Tierney Kuhn、Matt Guthrie和Matt Elliott，感谢他们始终如一地与模型进行深思熟虑且能干的工作，并分享了详细的笔记和反馈。我们感谢Julian Michael、Geoffrey Irving、Javier Rando、Ajeya Cotra、William Saunders、Jan Leike和Chenhao Tan提供的反馈和讨论。
